commitMessage:
Bug 574532 - [dogfooding][cleanup] Use new StringBuffer -> StringBuilder
cleanup

To ensure that the new JDT cleanup works correctly for clients we should
run it also on our own code base. This help to ensure that the cleanup
works fine and helps us to cleanup our own code base.

From the commit message of the cleanup:

- create a cleanup to replace usage of StringBuffer with
  StringBuilder.  The two are equivalent, but StringBuffer is
  thread-safe and synchronized which makes it slower than
  StringBuilder which was introduced in Java 1.5.


To avoid API changes, we should only use the local variable change.

Change-Id: I5d2500423ea2fd37f6ef84d892ee7bc5cc8d19a0
Signed-off-by: Lars Vogel <Lars.Vogel@vogella.com>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/182587
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
duplicateOf: new StringBuffer() -> new StringBuilder() for local variables
categoryReason: The change directly addresses a performance improvement by replacing a synchronized class with an unsynchronized one when thread-safety is not required.
summary: This commit replaces `StringBuffer` with `StringBuilder` for local variable initializations. `StringBuilder` is a more performant, unsynchronized alternative suitable when thread-safety is not required. The transformation is guarded by `isAssignedToLocalVariable()` to ensure semantic correctness. This is a GREEN transformation, already covered by existing rules in `stringbuffer-to-stringbuilder.sandbox-hint`.

Bug 574039 - M1 Merge branch 'master' into BETA_JAVA17

# Conflicts:
#	org.eclipse.jdt.compiler.apt/src/org/eclipse/jdt/internal/compiler/apt/util/ArchiveFileObject.java

Change-Id: Ic051bdcd74697c3b0b58fa26b2290f4152f2eb1d
categoryReason: The replacement of StringBuffer with StringBuilder is a standard performance optimization for non-thread-safe string manipulation.
summary: This commit replaces multiple instances of `java.lang.StringBuffer` with `java.lang.StringBuilder` in various JDT Core DOM classes. This is a common performance optimization, as `StringBuilder` is unsynchronized and faster than `StringBuffer` when thread safety is not a concern. The changes observed are for local variables, where `StringBuilder` is safe to use. This transformation pattern is already covered by existing DSL rules in `stringbuffer-to-stringbuilder.sandbox-hint`, which use the `isAssignedToLocalVariable()` guard to ensure semantic correctness, preventing changes where thread-safety might be required.

commitMessage:
Bug 574586 - [dogfooding][cleanup] Use try-with-resource cleanup

Change-Id: Ib4a64d26190545f489891c5a4197e26f7ac31410
Signed-off-by: Joerg Kubitz <jkubitz-eclipse@gmx.de>
Reviewed-on: https://git.eclipse.org/r/c/jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/+/182672
Reviewed-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
Tested-by: JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
Tested-by: Jeff Johnston <jjohnstn@redhat.com>
categoryReason: This commit modernizes resource management by converting a manual `try-finally` block into a `try-with-resources` statement.
languageChangeNeeded: The DSL needs to support structural statement transformations, specifically converting a `try-finally` block managing an `AutoCloseable` resource into a `try-with-resources` statement. This involves parsing and rewriting entire statement blocks, including moving resource initialization into the try-header and removing the `finally` block, which is currently not supported for complex control flow replacements.
summary: This commit converts a manual `try-finally` block that explicitly closes a `PrintWriter` into a more concise and safer `try-with-resources` statement. While this is a common and highly beneficial modernization pattern for `AutoCloseable` resources, the current TriggerPattern DSL does not support complex structural rewrites of entire statement blocks involving control flow changes (e.g., transforming a `try-finally` into a `try-with-resources` statement). Therefore, it requires significant DSL language extensions to be implemented as an automated transformation rule.
